meta.certificationName: AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate (MLA-C01)
meta.title: AWS-MLE-1: SageMaker Preparation, MLOps and Monitoring

domains.name: Deployment and Orchestration of ML Workflows

questions.stem: A team needs to **run managed preprocessing and evaluation code before training**. Which service or feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**B** is correct because it is the managed capability for preprocessing, feature engineering, and evaluation workloads in SageMaker

**A** is incorrect because it is not the best fit for offline batch scoring of large datasets

**C** is incorrect because it is not the AWS feature intended for automated hyperparameter search

**D** is incorrect because it is not the AWS feature intended for post-deployment drift and quality monitoring

[SageMaker Processing jobs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/processing-job.html)

questions.stem:
Which service or feature is most directly associated with the following need?

- Goal: run managed preprocessing and evaluation code before training
- Constraint: use the managed vendor capability when possible

What should the team choose?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because it is the managed capability for preprocessing, feature engineering, and evaluation workloads in SageMaker

**B** is incorrect because it is not the best fit for offline batch scoring of large datasets

**C** is incorrect because it is not the AWS feature intended for automated hyperparameter search

**D** is incorrect because it is not the AWS feature intended for post-deployment drift and quality monitoring

[SageMaker Processing jobs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/processing-job.html)

questions.stem: A practitioner is reviewing platform options. When should they choose **SageMaker Processing**?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because teams should choose it when they need to run managed preprocessing and evaluation code before training

**B** is incorrect because it is not the AWS feature intended for post-deployment drift and quality monitoring

**C** is incorrect because it is not the AWS feature intended for automated hyperparameter search

**D** is incorrect because it is not the best fit for offline batch scoring of large datasets

[SageMaker Processing jobs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/processing-job.html)

questions.stem: A team needs to **store reusable ML features for both training and online inference**. Which service or feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**D** is correct because it provides managed online and offline feature storage to reduce training-serving skew

**A** is incorrect because it is not the primary orchestration service for end-to-end SageMaker ML steps

**B** is incorrect because it is not the AWS feature intended for post-deployment drift and quality monitoring

**C** is incorrect because it does not primarily provide experiment tracking across model-development runs

[SageMaker Feature Store](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/feature-store.html)

questions.stem: A team needs to **crawl schemas and run serverless ETL over raw training data**. Which service or feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**B** is correct because it provides crawlers, ETL jobs, and a metadata catalog for serverless data preparation

**A** is incorrect because it does not primarily provide experiment tracking across model-development runs

**C** is incorrect because it is not the primary orchestration service for end-to-end SageMaker ML steps

**D** is incorrect because it is not the AWS feature intended for post-deployment drift and quality monitoring

questions.stem: A team needs to **compare training runs, parameters, metrics, and artifacts**. Which service or feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**D** is correct because it tracks training runs and related metadata so teams can compare experiments reproducibly

**A** is incorrect because it is not the main SageMaker capability for bias and explainability analysis

**B** is incorrect because it is not the AWS feature intended for post-deployment drift and quality monitoring

**C** is incorrect because it is not the primary orchestration service for end-to-end SageMaker ML steps

questions.stem: A team needs to **search hyperparameter ranges against an objective metric**. Which service or feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because it is the managed hyperparameter optimization feature in SageMaker

**B** is incorrect because it does not act as the central online/offline feature repository for SageMaker ML features

**C** is incorrect because it is not the primary AWS service for serverless ETL cataloging in this ML-preparation scenario

**D** is incorrect because it does not provide versioned model approval and governance for promotion

[SageMaker automatic model tuning](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/automatic-model-tuning.html)

**D** is incorrect because it does not act as the central online/offline feature repository for SageMaker ML features

questions.stem: A team needs to **orchestrate processing, training, evaluation, and registration steps**. Which service or feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**C** is correct because it is the managed orchestration layer for repeatable end-to-end ML workflows in SageMaker

**A** is incorrect because it does not primarily provide experiment tracking across model-development runs

**B** is incorrect because it does not act as the central online/offline feature repository for SageMaker ML features

**D** is incorrect because it serves a different ML lifecycle need than managed preprocessing jobs

[SageMaker Pipelines](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/pipelines.html)

questions.stem: A team needs to **score large datasets offline without a persistent endpoint**. Which service or feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because it runs offline inference jobs over datasets such as files in Amazon S3

**B** is incorrect because it is not the AWS feature intended for automated hyperparameter search

**C** is incorrect because it does not primarily provide experiment tracking across model-development runs

**D** is incorrect because it is not the primary orchestration service for end-to-end SageMaker ML steps

[SageMaker Batch Transform](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/batch-transform.html)

questions.stem: A team needs to **detect drift and quality issues after deployment**. Which service or feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because it profiles baselines and monitors deployed models for data and model quality issues

**B** is incorrect because it is not the best fit for offline batch scoring of large datasets

**C** is incorrect because it is not the main SageMaker capability for bias and explainability analysis

**D** is incorrect because it serves a different ML lifecycle need than managed preprocessing jobs

[SageMaker Model Monitor](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/model-monitor.html)
